mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held at or above 4.5% while CPIAUCSL is still elevated, markets infer that policy stays restrictive and real rates remain positive; this lifts term premia and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS >= 4.5 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation remains above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by falling 10-year yields in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 20-day yield change is negative despite elevated policy and inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE with slowing PAYEMS indicates weakening labor demand and lower wage pressure; investors then price slower growth and lower future rates, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if labor-softening triggers are not followed by lower 10-year yields in at least 55% of cases, or if the mean 20-day yield change is positive across triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A broad sales and activity uptrend in RSAFS and INDPRO signals firmer nominal demand and stronger real activity, which raises expected policy path and term premium; the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 1-month trends in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to precede positive 5-day yield changes, or if the sign hit rate falls below 55% over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST after an already weak GDPC1 print indicates housing-led demand deterioration and slower future inflation; the market responds by pushing the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% from its 3-month average and the latest GDPC1 growth print is below 2% annualized
failure_condition: Reject if housing contractions combined with weak GDP are not followed by lower yields in a majority of cases, or if the average 20-day yield change is non-negative after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5% but CPIAUCSL stops decelerating, the market reads policy as likely to remain tight for longer, which sustains upward pressure on the 10-year Treasury yield over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5 and CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation trend is flat or rising
failure_condition: Reject if the 60-day average yield change after the trigger is not positive, or if yields fall in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS gains indicates resilient labor demand, supporting stronger consumption and wage pressure; this lifts expected inflation and makes the 10-year Treasury yield rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if resilient labor conditions do not correspond to higher 20-day yields in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean yield change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while HOUST also falls, the combination points to broad production softness and supply-side slack; investors infer lower inflation pressure and bid the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months and HOUST is below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weakness in production and housing does not precede lower 5-day yields in a majority of triggered cases, or if the sign accuracy stays below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle slowdown, weakening retail sales and payroll growth signal softer nominal demand and less future inflation, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 60 trading days even if near-term policy rates are unchanged.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of slowing RSAFS and PAYEMS are not followed by lower 60-day yields, or if the 60-day conditional mean yield change is positive over any rolling 3-year window.
